summary: Deliver the example feature as two approval-ready, commit-worthy slices.

requirements.text: Land the first behavior change in an isolated slice.
Land the follow-up behavior change in a second isolated slice.
Each slice declares the command that verifies it before approval.

assumptions: The existing example modules are the correct extension points.
out_of_scope: Additional refactors beyond the two verified slices.

steps.title: Add the first verified slice
steps.goal: Land the first commit-worthy behavior change.
constraints: Keep the diff scoped to the first slice.
steps.justification: This isolates the first behavior change so review and rollback remain simple.
risk_flags: Consumer behavior must remain stable for current callers.
rollback_notes: Revert the step 1 commit if the first verified slice regresses current callers.
operational_watchpoints: Watch the example module public behavior contract during review and verification.

steps.title: Land the follow-up slice
steps.goal: Complete the second commit-worthy behavior change.
constraints: Keep the follow-up scoped to the second slice.
steps.justification: This lands the second behavior change without reopening the first slice.
interfaces_to_preserve: Second example module public behavior contract
risk_flags: Step 1 behavior must remain intact after the follow-up slice.
rollback_notes: Revert only the step 2 commit if the follow-up slice destabilizes the first slice.
operational_watchpoints: Watch downstream consumers of the second example module while step 2 lands.
